title: orthilia secunda var. elatior
intro: Orthilia secunda var. elatior, also known as Mountain Cranberry or Elate Mountain Cranberry, is a perennial plant in the heath family that is native to the mountains of Europe. The medicinal properties of Orthilia secunda var. elatior lie in its leaves and roots, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce pain, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Orthilia secunda var. elatior include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory problems, gastrointestinal issues, and skin irritations.
